articles.headline: Romania great Gheorghe Hagi returns to coach national team
articles.description: Romania great Gheorghe Hagi has been hired as national team coach for the second time. He was appointed to replace Mircea Lucescu, who died two weeks ago.

images.name: Gheorghe Hagi Romania coach_260421 [1024x576]
images.caption: Gheorghe Hagi agreed to coach the Romania national team for the second time in his career following a brief stint in 2001.
images.alt: Gheorghe Hagi agreed to coach the Romania national team for the second time in his career following a brief stint in 2001.

articles.headline: Rosenior calls for behaviour improvement after Chelsea's ninth red card
articles.description: Liam Rosenior describes Chelsea's indiscipline this season as a "learning experience" following Pedro Neto's red card vs. Arsenal and looks ahead to visiting Aston Villa.

images.name: Rosenior calls for behaviour improvement after Chelsea's ninth red card
images.caption: Liam Rosenior describes Chelsea's indiscipline this season as a "learning experience" following Pedro Neto's red card vs. Arsenal and looks ahead to visiting Aston Villa.

articles.headline: Ireland to fulfil Nations League games with Israel despite call for ban
articles.description: The Republic of Ireland said they will fulfil their Nations League fixtures against Israel in the autumn despite calling for the country to be banned by UEFA last year.

images.name: Ireland football logo [1296x729]
images.caption: The Irish football federation will call on UEFA to ban Israel.
images.alt: he official badge of the Republic of Ireland national football team on a shirt during the FIFA Women's World Cup Australia & New Zealand 2023 Group F match between France and Jamaica at Sydney Football Stadium on July 23, 2023 in Sydney, Australia

articles.headline: Nations League draw: England get Spain, Croatia, Czechia
articles.description: England face a rematch with their Euro 2024 final conquerors Spain in the 2026-27 Nations League.

images.name: England vs Spain Euro 2024 final [1296x729]
images.caption: Jude Bellingham and England were beaten by Spain the final of Euro 2024.
images.alt: Jude Bellingham of England in action during the UEFA EURO 2024 final match between Spain and England at Olympiastadion on July 14, 2024 in Berlin, Germany.

images.name: Did Thomas Tuchel deserve a contract extension with England?
images.caption: ESPN's Nedum Onuoha and Julien Laurens react to Thomas Tuchel signing a new contract extension with England until 2028.

articles.headline: Will Sesko's brace vs. Burnley kick-start his Man United career?
articles.description: Stewart Robson talks about Benjamin Sesko's performance after scoring two goals in Manchester United 2-2 draw with Burnley.

images.name: Will Sesko's brace vs. Burnley kick-start his Man United career?
images.caption: Stewart Robson talks about Benjamin Sesko's performance after scoring two goals in Manchester United 2-2 draw with Burnley.

articles.headline: Kylian Mbappe's injury 'not good news' for Real Madrid's schedule

images.name: Kylian Mbappe's injury 'not good news' for Real Madrid's schedule

article.headline: Kylian Mbappe, Olivier Giroud lead France past Austria in Nations League
article.description: France kept their hopes of staying in the top tier of the Nations League alive with goals by Kylian Mbappe and Olivier Giroud in a win over Austria.
article.linkText: Mbappe, Giroud lead France to win over Austria

article.story:
France kept their hopes of staying in the top tier of the Nations League alive as goals by Kylian Mbappe and Olivier Giroud secured a 2-0 home victory over Austria on Thursday.

Mbappe opened the scoring with another stunning goal before Giroud, who again showed coach Didier Deschamps he is more than a third-choice forward, sealed the world champions' win with a header.

- World Cup stock watch: Who's hot, who's not ahead of Qatar

France, who travel to Denmark on Sunday, climbed to third in Group 1 with five points from as many games.

Victory came at a price, however, as defender Jules Kounde and goalkeeper Mike Maignan sustained injuries, and may join Karim Benzema, Hugo Lloris, Paul Pogba on the sidelines.

"We did what we had to do," the 1998 world champion said. "Our intentions were good, everyone defended a lot, there are a lot of positives. The mindset was good, it was a very good performance."

The hosts, who had lost two and drawn two of their four previous games, came out at top speed at the Stade de France.

Mbappe fired a shot into the top corner in the second minute, but the effort was disallowed for offside.

Aurelien Tchouameni's attempt from just outside the box after a quick combination between Antoine Griezmann and Mbappe was tipped over by Austria keeper Patrick Pentz as Les Bleus piled on the pressure.

Defender Kounde became the latest casualty in an injury-ravaged squad when he limped off the pitch with a muscle problem and Maignan was replaced by Alphonse Areola at halftime due to a calf pain.

Mbappe, denied by Pentz after a one-two with Giroud, tormented the Austrian defence with his pace and created space for his teammates, Jonathan Clauss just missing out on a goal early in the second half.

Mbappe broke the deadlock with a cool finish after beating five defenders following a perfect pass from Giroud.

It spurred Austria into action, but they lacked accuracy in the box and it was France who scored again.

Giroud found the back of the net in the 65th minute from Griezmann's pinpoint cross for his 49th international goal, two shy of Thierry Henry's national record, while also becoming the oldest player to ever score for France.

The AC Milan striker was replaced by Ousmane Dembele to the Stade de France crowd's standing ovation, leaving Deschamps, who has only called up Giroud in the absence of Benzema, with a tricky decision before November's World Cup.

"Oliver deserves it, I am very happy for him. He had difficult periods with his club but always performed well with us. If I call him, it's so he can score goals. And he had many opportunities," Deschamps said after the match.

However, the French coach urged caution as it regarded his decision to include Giroud in his final squad for Qatar, where they will begin against Group D play against Australia on Nov. 22.

Deschamps said: "Do not put words in my mouth. I did not say that he would be there. There is competition for every position and I can only take 26 players."
